title: Command-generation extraction epic decomposition
larger_intended_outcome: Extract command-generation from Agentic Workspace as an independently installable maintainer/source-checkout package while AW remains the host for command meaning, generated outputs, domain primitives, runtime-boundary policy, and packaging proof.
non_goals: Do not close GitHub #1201 from a partial slice or planning artifact alone.
Do not make command-generation a shipped AW runtime dependency.
Do not turn the package into a broad general-purpose CLI framework.
Do not move AW command contracts, generated outputs, or domain primitive meaning into command-generation.

candidate_lanes.title: #1201 Extract command-generation as an independent maintainer dependency
candidate_lanes.outcome: Coordinate the full extraction epic, enforce runtime dependency boundaries, and close only after all child lanes have proof.
candidate_lanes.proof: All child lane proofs pass; AW runtime packaging excludes command-generation; PR closeout maps every #1201 acceptance criterion.

candidate_lanes.title: #1202 Define command-generation public API and host manifest
candidate_lanes.outcome: AW consumes command-generation through a small public API and host manifest rather than renderer internals.
candidate_lanes.proof: Public API tests pass and AW generation uses public command_generation APIs only.

candidate_lanes.title: #1203 Move generic codegen schemas toward package ownership
candidate_lanes.outcome: Generic command package schemas have command-generation-owned paths and explicit AW resolution or mirror checks.
candidate_lanes.proof: Package schema tests and AW generated package checks load package-owned schemas without AW contract-tool imports.

candidate_lanes.title: #1204 Add custom primitive registry and target support contract
candidate_lanes.outcome: Host/domain primitives are schema-backed, registry-validated, target-supported, or explicitly unsupported.
candidate_lanes.proof: Primitive conformance covers registered, supported, unsupported, and unregistered cases.

candidate_lanes.title: #1205 Remove AW-specific branches from command-generation renderers
candidate_lanes.outcome: Generic renderers no longer hardcode AW package ids, command ids, payload lists, or resource conventions.
candidate_lanes.proof: Static coupling checks plus generated AW freshness/conformance pass after at least one AW-specific branch is removed or converted to data/IR.

candidate_lanes.title: #1206 Add non-AW command-generation fixture package
candidate_lanes.outcome: A small non-AW fixture renders and runs through command-generation public APIs without AW imports, paths, or command names.
candidate_lanes.proof: Command-generation tests render the fixture, execute a portable operation, and cover a custom primitive supported or unsupported case.

candidate_lanes.title: #1207 Convert AW generation and checks to command-generation package APIs
candidate_lanes.outcome: AW scripts are host wrappers around command_generation APIs, with only AW-specific policy remaining in AW.
candidate_lanes.proof: AW generation/check wrappers use package APIs, guard tests reject internal imports, and generated-output freshness passes.

candidate_lanes.title: #1208 Prepare external command-generation dependency adoption
candidate_lanes.outcome: AW uses command-generation as an external maintainer dependency while installed AW runtime remains independent of it.
candidate_lanes.proof: AW generation/checks work from the git dependency, AW runtime package metadata excludes command-generation, and both repos pass their tests.

dependency_assumptions: #1202 is the first implementation seam; later lanes should not bypass the public API/manifest boundary.
#1203 and #1204 can be shaped together, but custom primitives need a stable manifest/API surface.
#1205 and #1206 can proceed in parallel once API and primitive registry boundaries are stable.
#1208 is last because adopting the external dependency before fixture and AW wrapper proof would lock in hidden coupling.
parallelization_assumptions: Schema ownership and primitive registry work can be reviewed independently if both consume the same host manifest contract.
Renderer decoupling and non-AW fixture work can proceed in parallel after public API and registry contracts are ready.
External dependency adoption should remain serial and final.
proof_expectations: Each lane must run the narrow package tests relevant to its owner surface and the AW generated package freshness/conformance checks when AW outputs are affected.
Closeout for #1201 requires a parent acceptance map across #1202-#1208, not just a merged extraction PR.
Any dogfooding improvement discovered during execution must be fixed immediately if it blocks this epic or filed as a follow-up issue otherwise.
Runtime package proof must continue to show command-generation is absent from AW shipped dependencies.
promotion_rule: All eight GitHub lanes are promoted into execplans. Execute only dependency-ready lanes, keep validation local to each lane, and do not close the parent until all child completion criteria and runtime dependency proof are satisfied.
notes: Created after dogfooding showed the eight ingested GitHub candidates had been implemented against directly without first promoting them into durable decomposition and execplan surfaces. Follow-up guardrail filed as GitHub #1215.
